reasoning:
The resolution requires predicting if the number of 'Riots' in Dominica from Feb 20, 2026, to Mar 21, 2026, will be greater than the past 360-day average leading up to Mar 15, 2026. The 360-day average includes multiple violent clashes and riots on March 19-20, 2025. This yields a non-zero threshold (likely around 0.16 to 0.5 riots per month), so determining if the current 30-day window exceeds that threshold means there needs to be at least 1 riot in this 30-day period.

I have searched extensively for any riots or protests in Dominica between February 20, 2026, and today, March 15, 2026. I used various search terms (riot, protest, unrest, clash, tear gas, mob) and even checked the most prominent local online news source, dominicanewsonline.com. There is exactly zero evidence of any protests or riots in this window.

Given that 24 of the 30 days have already elapsed without incident, a riot would have to occur in the remaining 6 days (March 16-21, 2026) for the condition to be met. Given that Dominica is a small island nation with a population around 72,000, and riots only happen roughly every few years (with the major recent ones being Nov 2019, Oct 2024, and Mar 2025), the base rate of a riot happening in any random 6-day window is very low. Assuming at most 2 riots a year, the baseline for a 6-day window would be around 3%. Without any preceding political tension currently reported in the news that would act as a precursor, there's no reason to elevate the chance above the base rate. I estimate the probability to be around 4%.
